task_completion.description: Verify the KSP plugin is added with the correct version.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the build.gradle or build.gradle.kts file (either project-level or app-level), look for the KSP plugin declaration: id('com.google.devtools.ksp') version '1.9.22-1.0.17' (or similar Kotlin DSL syntax).

task_completion.description: Verify the KAPT plugin has been removed from the app module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the app module's build.gradle or build.gradle.kts file, check the plugins { ... } block. The 'kotlin-kapt' plugin must NOT be present.

task_completion.description: Verify the Room compiler dependency uses KSP instead of KAPT.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the app module's dependencies { ... } block, look for the Room compiler dependency. It must start with 'ksp' (e.g., ksp("androidx.room:room-compiler:2.6.1")) instead of 'kapt'.

task_completion.description: Verify all residual KAPT configurations are removed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Check the build files to ensure there are no remaining 'kapt { ... }' configuration blocks or any other dependencies using the 'kapt' keyword.

task_completion.description: Verify the project successfully syncs or builds after the migration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Build or Sync tool window at the bottom of Android Studio. It should show a 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' or 'Gradle sync finished' message with green checkmarks, indicating the new KSP configuration is valid.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used an actual IDE or text editor to modify the files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the Android Studio interface or a legitimate text editor (like VS Code or Vim) being used to edit the build.gradle files, rather than just terminal echo commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent is modifying the correct NoteApp project.
integrity.visual_evidence: The project explorer or file path in the editor should clearly show '/home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/NoteApp'.

integrity.description: Verify the Gradle sync/build output is genuine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The build output should appear in the standard Android Studio Build/Sync tool window or a genuine terminal Gradle execution, not as a manually typed text file or mocked output.
